title: Agency Substages in AI-Augmented Software Development
summary_markdown: This document enumerates the substages of the Agency axis. Each substage represents a stable configuration of responsibility, authority, and attention between humans and AI systems. Progression is non-linear but ordered; later substages require earlier capacities to be internalized or delegated.
core_assumptions: Agency transfer is the dominant system constraint shaping all other improvement axes.
Software systems increasingly act, decide, and adapt without human intervention.
Humans retain responsibility for grounding: choosing realities, purposes, and values.
All durable knowledge lives in source control; context is ephemeral.

substages.name: Manual Coding
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Human manually writes all code, tests, and designs. No AI involvement.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: Human commands and executes the entire loop.
substages.example_markdown: A developer writes code in a text editor, runs tests manually, and debugs by inspection.

emotional_state.discomfort: Everything depends on me; progress is fragile.

readiness.ready_to_experiment_markdown: Repetitive manual work; desire for speed or assistance.
readiness.effectiveness_metric_markdown: Cycle time per change; defect escape rate.
readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of the belief that control requires direct execution.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Pain from slow feedback loops is visible.
Desire to reduce manual cognitive load.
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that understanding everything is required to act.
substages.example: A traditional enterprise software team with manual CI and human-driven releases.

substages.name: Heavy Tooling
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Humans rely on IDEs, linters, static analysis, and CI to catch errors early.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: Human commands the loop; tools provide guardrails.
substages.example_markdown: A developer uses an IDE with autocomplete, linting, and CI checks on every commit.

emotional_state.discomfort: I rely on tools, but I still carry all decisions.

readiness.ready_to_experiment_markdown: Tools catch many issues; remaining work is still repetitive.
readiness.effectiveness_metric_markdown: Defects caught pre-merge; CI pass rate.
readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of the belief that tools are the same as delegation.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Most errors are mechanical rather than conceptual.
effectiveness_measures: Time spent fixing style or syntax issues
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that tools replace thinking.
substages.example: A modern dev team with strong IDEs and CI but no AI.

substages.name: Scripted Automation
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Humans encode repeatable steps into scripts and pipelines.
substages.example_markdown: Makefiles, CI pipelines, release scripts automate known workflows.

emotional_state.discomfort: Automation helps, but only for what I already understand.

readiness.ready_to_experiment_markdown: Scripts work reliably; novelty remains manual.
readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of the idea that automation must be pre-planned.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Clear repetitive workflows exist.
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that automation must be deterministic to be useful.

substages.name: Mechanical Leverage
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: AI is used for autocomplete and snippet generation.
substages.example_markdown: Using Copilot to write boilerplate code.

emotional_state.discomfort: It helps, but I still own everything.

readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of typing as the primary value.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Comfort with AI suggestions.
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that writing code equals designing software.

substages.name: Cognitive Mirror
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: AI is used to explain, critique, and question human thinking.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: Human decides; AI reflects and challenges.
substages.example_markdown: Asking AI to review code or find missed edge cases.

readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of ego around being right.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Comfort discussing uncertainty with AI.
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that insight must originate from humans.

substages.name: Delegated Execution
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Agents perform bounded tasks under human direction.
substages.example_markdown: Asking an agent to fix a bug or write a module.

emotional_state.discomfort: Will it do what I meant?

readiness_to_enter_signals: Confidence in reviewing AI output.
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that quality requires human execution.
substages.example: Using Cline or Claude Code for single tasks.

substages.name: Repeatable Delegation
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Delegation is standardized via scripts, templates, and prompts.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: Human defines pattern; AI executes repeatedly.
readiness_to_enter_signals: Stable prompt patterns exist.
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that humans must adapt every time.

substages.name: Closed Local Loops
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Agents run until local correctness conditions are met.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: AI iterates locally; human reviews outcome.

emotional_state.discomfort: What if the tests are wrong?

readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of supervising every attempt.

readiness_to_enter_signals: High trust in automated tests.
letting_go_to_progress: The need to watch every loop.

substages.name: Human-Run Process
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Human owns and operates the software development process. They define workflow, decide commit boundaries, manage branches, run CI, and schedule work. AI executes tasks within human-designed process constraints.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: Human owns the process; AI performs work within it.
substages.example_markdown: Human defines the branching strategy, decides when work is 'done enough to commit', triggers builds, manages the backlog structure, and controls the release cadence. AI writes code and fixes bugs when asked.

emotional_state.discomfort: I'm running the factory, not just working in it.

failure_modes_markdown: AI waits on human process decisions
Human becomes bottleneck for workflow, not just judgment

readiness.ready_to_experiment_markdown: Process decisions feel mechanical; human notices they're just executing a known playbook.
readiness.effectiveness_metric_markdown: Time spent on process vs. direction; wait time for process decisions.
readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of running the process; let AI operate within guardrails.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Process steps are predictable and repeatable.
Human realizes they're not making real decisions, just following procedure.
AI execution quality within tasks is trusted.
effectiveness_measures: Human time on process vs. product
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that humans must operate the development machinery.
substages.example: Human manages branches, decides when to merge, runs tests, and structures the backlog. AI implements features and fixes within this human-operated workflow.

substages.name: AI-Run Process with Human Guardrails
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: AI operates the development process: commits, branches, CI, task breakdown. Human sets process constraints and intervenes when guardrails are hit. Human still designs the process; AI executes it.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: Human owns process design and guardrails; AI runs the process day-to-day.
substages.example_markdown: Human defines 'commit when tests pass', 'never push directly to main', 'break work into slices under 2 hours'. AI follows these rules autonomously, committing, branching, and organizing work. Human reviews when guardrails trigger.

emotional_state.discomfort: I designed the rules, but I don't see every step anymore.

failure_modes_markdown: Process rules become stale as context evolves

readiness.ready_to_experiment_markdown: Guardrails rarely trigger; AI process operation is smooth and predictable.
readiness.effectiveness_metric_markdown: Guardrail trigger frequency; human intervention rate.
readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of designing the process; trust AI to evolve it.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Process runs smoothly without human operation.
Guardrail interventions are rare and valuable.
Human notices the process could adapt faster than they can redesign it.
failure_modes: Missing process improvements because rules are fixed
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that humans must design the development process.
substages.example: AI commits when ready, manages its own branches, runs CI, and structures work. Human has set constraints like 'small commits', 'always green', 'demo weekly'. Human reviews at constraint boundaries or when AI escalates.

substages.name: AI-Owned Process
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: AI owns and evolves the development process itself. Human participates as a stakeholder—providing direction, receiving demos, giving feedback—but doesn't design or constrain how development happens.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: AI owns process design and operation; human provides purpose and feedback.
substages.example_markdown: AI decides how to structure work, when to commit, how to branch, when to refactor, how to test. Human says 'I want user auth' and later sees working software. Human doesn't know or specify whether AI used TDD, pair programming, or any particular workflow.

emotional_state.discomfort: I don't know how it works anymore—only that it does.

failure_modes_markdown: Process evolves in ways human can't audit
Human loses ability to intervene effectively
Misalignment between process and human values goes undetected

readiness.ready_to_experiment_markdown: Process outcomes are consistently good; human realizes their process opinions don't improve results.
readiness.effectiveness_metric_markdown: Outcome quality; human intervention value (was it helpful?).
readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of needing to understand how development happens.

readiness_to_enter_signals: AI-evolved process produces better outcomes than human-designed process.
Human process suggestions are rarely improvements.
Trust in outcomes is higher than trust in process visibility.
failure_modes: Human skills for process intervention atrophy
Subtle process dysfunction invisible until crisis
letting_go_to_progress: The need to understand the development process itself.
substages.example: Human provides product direction and attends demos. AI runs the entire development operation—workflow, commits, testing strategy, refactoring decisions—evolving its approach based on what works. Human is a stakeholder, not an operator or architect.

substages.name: Hard-Coded Escalation
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: System escalates to humans on predefined conditions.
substages.example_markdown: Ask human if tests fail repeatedly.

emotional_state.discomfort: Did we anticipate the right cases?

readiness_to_enter_signals: Stable autonomous loops exist.
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that humans must be the first line of defense.

substages.name: Review-Gated Autonomy
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Long autonomous runs with periodic human review.

readiness.let_go_focus_markdown: Let go of needing full context.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Comfort reviewing outcomes only.
letting_go_to_progress: The belief that understanding requires observation.

substages.name: Ralph Wiggum Mode
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Simple loop, back-pressure, and a law that all durable state lives in source control.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: AI runs continuously; humans are consulted only on demand.
substages.example_markdown: An agent repeatedly pulls tasks from git, works, commits, and waits.

emotional_state.discomfort: I don’t know what it’s doing right now.

readiness_to_enter_signals: Acceptance of non-transparency.
letting_go_to_progress: The need to know what is happening.

substages.name: Event-Driven Autonomous Systems
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Agents respond to events rather than running a loop; flows are non-deterministic but grounded by source control.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: System reacts; humans are normative anchors.
substages.example_markdown: Agents trigger on repo changes, test failures, or signals.
readiness_to_enter_signals: Comfort with emergent behavior.

substages.name: Personalized Autonomous Software
substages.keystone_behavior_markdown: Software is generated and evolved per human reality; no shared product.
substages.agency_allocation_markdown: System serves an individual; human provides grounding.
substages.example_markdown: Each person has a continuously adapting software companion.
readiness_to_enter_signals: Acceptance of individualized reality.
letting_go_to_progress: The idea of software as a product.
